title: Canada Social Insurance Number (SIN) Application
description: Apply for, confirm, or amend a Social Insurance Number (SIN), Canada's tax/benefits/employment identifier, using the data model of Form GC-NAS2120 (Application for a Social Insurance Number). No fee. Fastest channel is online (10 business days); by mail using this form (20 business days); or in person at a Service Canada Centre, where this form is not required and the SIN is issued at the visit. Every applicant must provide original, valid identity documents (photocopies are not accepted); this document models the biographic/mailing-address data collected on the form itself, not the identity-document verification process. It does not submit the application; the live Employment and Social Development Canada / Service Canada source is always authoritative (see VERIFICATION.md).

authority.name: Employment and Social Development Canada, Service Canada

verification.notes: Source-derived reference schema transcribed field-by-field from the live Form GC-NAS2120 PDF at catalogue.servicecanada.gc.ca (fetched directly, no access block). All 8 pages (the Information Guide for Applicants, the application form itself — items 1-13 — and the mail-application checklist) extracted as clean, legible text and transcribed field-by-field. Not a time-versioned/edition form: a SIN application is a one-time filing (a SIN is issued once per lifetime; amendments use the same form/process rather than a new period-specific edition), so it is authored at the plain (non-edition) registry path per the GSP-0005 §2 coexistence test. Status remains 'draft' pending full independent field-by-field source review. See VERIFICATION.md.

fields.name: applicationType
fields.label: I am applying for a(n)
fields.description: Select one. legal_change_of_name and change_to_expiry_date_900_series refer to a name legally changed since the last SIN record, and to updating a temporary ("900 series") SIN's expiry date, respectively.

fields.name: otherApplicationTypeSpecify
fields.label: If "other", specify
fields.description: Required when applicationType is other.

fields.name: firstGivenName
fields.label: First given name (to be shown on SIN record)
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.

fields.name: otherGivenNames
fields.label: Other given name(s) (to be shown on SIN record)
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.

fields.name: familyName
fields.label: Family name (to be shown on SIN record)
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.

fields.name: dateOfBirth
fields.label: Applicant's date of birth
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.

fields.name: gender
fields.label: Applicant's gender
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Optional — the Information Guide states applicants "must fully complete items 1 to 13, except gender information (in item 3), which is optional." Item 3 has three radio-button options on the form itself: Male, Female, and X (the federal "X" sex designation available on Canadian ID/documents since 2019).

fields.name: isMultipleBirth
fields.label: Is the applicant a twin, triplet, etc.?
fields.description: Optional check box on the form.

fields.name: parent1GivenNames
fields.label: Applicant's parent's given name(s) (name at birth)
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.

fields.name: parent1FamilyNameAtBirth
fields.label: Applicant's parent's family name at birth
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.

fields.name: parent2GivenNames
fields.label: Applicant's second parent's given name(s) (name at birth)
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Item 5 is one of items 1-13, which the Information Guide (Step 2) requires be "fully complete" except gender (item 3) — the sole named exception. The parent listed in item 4 must not be repeated here; parents can appear in either order. Some provinces recognize up to four parents on a birth registration — additional parents beyond items 4/5 are attached on a separate sheet per the form's own instruction, out of scope for this flat field set (see GSP-0009).

fields.name: parent2FamilyNameAtBirth
fields.label: Applicant's second parent's family name at birth
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Item 5 is one of items 1-13, which the Information Guide (Step 2) requires be "fully complete" except gender (item 3) — the sole named exception.

fields.name: placeOfBirthCity
fields.label: Applicant's place of birth — city, town, or village
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.

fields.name: placeOfBirthProvinceOrState
fields.label: Applicant's place of birth — province/territory/state
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.

fields.name: placeOfBirthCountry
fields.label: Applicant's place of birth — country
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.

fields.name: familyNameAtBirth
fields.label: Applicant's family name at birth
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Distinct from familyName (item 1), which is the name to be shown on the SIN record and may differ (e.g. after marriage).

fields.name: otherFamilyNamesPreviouslyUsed
fields.label: Other family name(s) previously used
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.

fields.name: hadPreviousSin
fields.label: Did the applicant ever have a Social Insurance Number (SIN)?
fields.description: yes_with_number = provide previousSinNumber. no = never had one. unknown = don't recall.

fields.name: previousSinNumber
fields.label: Previous SIN, if known
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Required when hadPreviousSin is yes_with_number. 9 digits, no separators.

fields.name: statusInCanada
fields.label: Applicant's status in Canada
fields.description: Select one. Determines which identity documents are required (see Step 1 of the Information Guide) — not modeled here as separate fields, since this document covers the application form's own biographic/mailing fields, not the identity-verification document set.

fields.name: currentlyResidingInCanada
fields.label: Is the applicant currently residing in Canada?
fields.description: Sub-question directly beneath the status-in-Canada checkboxes.

fields.name: primaryPhoneNumber
fields.label: Primary telephone number
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. In international E.164 format, e.g. +18665551234.

fields.name: eveningPhoneNumber
fields.label: Evening telephone number
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. In international E.164 format.

fields.name: mailingAddressInCareOf
fields.label: In care of (if different from the applicant's name)
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.

fields.name: mailingAddressStreet
fields.label: Mailing address — number and street
fields.description: The SIN confirmation letter is mailed to this address.

fields.name: mailingAddressAptSuiteUnit
fields.label: Mailing address — apartment, suite, or unit number
fields.description: If applicable.

fields.name: mailingAddressCity
fields.label: Mailing address — city, town, or village
fields.description: The SIN confirmation letter is mailed to this address.

fields.name: mailingAddressProvinceOrState
fields.label: Mailing address — province/territory/state
fields.description: Two-letter Canadian province/territory abbreviation, or the foreign equivalent.

fields.name: mailingAddressCountry
fields.label: Mailing address — country
fields.description: May be outside Canada.

fields.name: mailingAddressPostalCode
fields.label: Mailing address — postal/ZIP code
fields.description: 5-digit ZIP or Canadian A1A 1A1 postal-code format, as applicable.

fields.name: signatureDate
fields.label: Date of signature
fields.description: The applicant signs, except: a parent/legal guardian signs for a child under 12; the child, parent, or legal guardian signs for a child 12 and under the age of majority; and a provincial/territorial employee, legal guardian, or court-appointed representative signs for a minor or adult they represent. If "X" is used as a signature, two witnesses must also sign and state their relationship to the applicant.

fields.name: representativeRelationship
fields.label: Relationship of representative to the applicant
fields.description: Required when the form is signed by someone other than the applicant. If given, also provide representativePrintedName.

fields.name: representativePrintedName
fields.label: Printed name of representative
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Required when representativeRelationship is given.

fields.name: representativePhoneNumber
fields.label: Telephone number of representative
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. In international E.164 format.

steps.title: Previous SIN and status in Canada
